name: Genome Nexus
description: description='Genome Nexus is a comprehensive resource designed for the fast, automated, and high-throughput annotation and interpretation of genetic variants in cancer. It integrates information from various existing databases to convert DNA changes to protein changes, predict the functional effects of protein mutations, and provide insights into mutation frequencies, gene functions, variant effects, and clinical actionability. The project aims to facilitate researchers and clinicians in understanding genetic variations and their implications in cancer, thereby enhancing the efficiency of genomic analysis and interpretation.'
highLevelGoals: Facilitate researchers in comprehensively understanding genetic variations and their implications in cancer to drive innovative research and discoveries.
Enable clinicians to make informed treatment decisions based on accurate interpretation of genetic data, ultimately improving patient outcomes.
Assist bioinformaticians in efficiently analyzing and interpreting complex genetic data to support research and clinical applications.
Support laboratory technicians in performing accurate and efficient laboratory tests and analyses related to genetic research, ensuring high-quality results.
lowLevelGoals: Allow researchers to fetch genetic variation data from the database using specific filters such as gene name, mutation type, and cancer type.
Enable researchers to submit a request for analysis of specific genetic variations to the analysis engine.
Allow clinicians to retrieve genetic profiles of patients to inform treatment decisions based on their specific genetic variations.
Enable clinicians to update treatment recommendations based on the latest genetic data interpretations.
Allow bioinformaticians to start a data analysis workflow for a specific dataset of genetic variations.
Enable bioinformaticians to fetch the results of completed genetic data analyses for review and interpretation.
Allow laboratory technicians to input and log results from genetic tests performed in the lab.
Enable laboratory technicians to request quality control checks on genetic test results to ensure accuracy and reliability.
